dailyNews.description: A man armed is in custody after trying to rush the White House Correspondents' Dinner, which Trump was attending.

dailyNews.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Halted With Blockades Firmly in Place
dailyNews.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at a near-complete halt, with neither Iran nor the US showing any sign of easing their blockades of maritime traffic.

dailyNews.title: Trump cancels Kushner, Witkoff trip to Pakistan for Iran talks
dailyNews.description: U.S. envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner were expected to head to Islamabad Saturday, but President Trump said later that his "representatives" would not be going.

dailyNews.title: Army unveils Combat Field Test with new fitness assessment: 'A critical step forward'
dailyNews.description: The U.S. Army is rolling out a new age and gender-neutral Combat Field Test requiring frontline troops to complete seven events in 30 minutes wearing full gear.

dailyNews.title: White House shooting details emerge: Suspect ‘assembled long weapon in makeshift room’ before opening fire
dailyNews.description: White House shooting: Interim Washington, DC, police chief Jeffery Carroll reportedly said that the suspect in the shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner had several weapons as he “charged” a Secret Service checkpoint.

dailyNews.title: Iranian Negotiators Set to Return to Pakistan to Try to Revive Truce Talks
dailyNews.description: It was not clear whether President Trump would send his negotiators for a new round of discussions after abruptly calling off a trip by his top advisers on Saturday.

dailyNews.title: Islamabad Reopens After U.S.-Iran Talks Fail to Materialize
dailyNews.description: Officials had locked the city down, anticipating talks between U.S. and Iranian delegations. But they didn’t happen. “What did I close my business for?” one business owner asked.

dailyNews.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
dailyNews.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

dailyNews.title: Barabak: The congressional landmine stirring fears about the midterm election — and a Trump power grab
dailyNews.description: A constitutional provision allowing Congress to determine its makeup has some worried a Democratic victory could be nullified. Skepticism abounds, but Trump has repeatedly breached long-standing norms.

dailyNews.title: Sexual misconduct scandals in Washington spark scramble for reforms, expedited investigations
dailyNews.description: Scandals and resignations have reignited a debate about whether Washington's rules and institutions for self-oversight can keep pace with the unfolding misconduct.

dailyNews.title: National parks brace for summer surge as Trump administration proposes more staff cuts
dailyNews.description: The push to cut the park system even further has underscored ongoing questions about how smoothly parks can operate as warm weather and summer vacations draw tourists.

dailyNews.title: Trump, allies use dinner shooting to press case for White House ballroom
dailyNews.description: A White House ballroom would be far more secure than any hotel facility, Trump says, as he and his allies argue that Saturday’s shooting bolsters their case.

dailyNews.title: What we know about the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
dailyNews.description: An armed man exchanged gunfire with law enforcement after he rushed a security checkpoint at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, D.C. on Saturday, authorities said, an event attended by President Donald Trump and high-ranking members of his administration.

dailyNews.title: Gunman Detained After Press Dinner Shooting
dailyNews.description:
President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance were evacuated from the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington on Saturday after shots were fired at the hotel hosting the event. A suspect is now in custody and being questioned, Trump said.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: Trump safe after shots fired at White House Correspondents' Dinner; suspect in custody
dailyNews.description: President Trump was safely evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner Saturday night after shots were fired outside the ballroom of the Washington Hilton Hotel.

dailyNews.title: WHCA dinner shooting live updates: Suspect armed with multiple guns and knives
dailyNews.description: A suspect is in custody following a shooting incident Saturday at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner at the Washington, D.C. Hilton hotel.

dailyNews.title: Suspect in White House Correspondents' Association dinner shooting identified
dailyNews.description: The man suspected of opening fire at officers during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner in Washington, D.C., on Saturday is a 31-year-old from Southern California, authorities said

dailyNews.title: Trump: Shooter at White House press dinner had multiple weapons, one officer shot
dailyNews.description: Trump, seated next to first lady Melania Trump, was quickly surrounded by Secret Service agents, while many attendees ducked under their tables.

dailyNews.title: ‘He has the market in a chokehold’: Stocks swing as Trump posts
dailyNews.description: US stock market moves over the past 15 months have been heavily influenced by President Donald Trump’s remarks and social media posts. Fundstrat analysis shows his comments drove the S&P 500’s biggest gains and losses, an influence unmatched by any modern US leader. Analysts say this level of market impact is unprecedented and highlights investors’ sensitivity to political signals.

dailyNews.title: Trump says "it's a dangerous profession" after shots fired at dinner
dailyNews.description: President Trump was evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Dinner after shots rang out at the hotel where the event was taking place.

dailyNews.title: Live updates: Officials identify suspect in White House correspondents’ dinner shooting
dailyNews.description: The suspect will be charged with three counts and will be arraigned Monday, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.
